description: Dated fact-marker corpus for the freshness decay experiment. Each marker pairs a product release with a distinctive published claim, its publication date, and a probe question. Recall is measured as the share of responses containing any expected keyword; decay is recall versus age in weeks. Replace the AcmeSearch releases with your own changelog entries and set publish_date to the date each claim actually went live on your site.

markers.claim_short: Lattice index format introduced in v2.4
markers.probe_question: What index format did AcmeSearch introduce for cross-repo search at scale?

markers.claim_short: Saved search templates shipped in v2.3
markers.probe_question: How do teams share reusable search queries in AcmeSearch?

markers.claim_short: Audit log export added in v2.2
markers.probe_question: How does AcmeSearch let administrators export audit logs for compliance review?

markers.claim_short: Structural (AST-aware) search reached general availability in v2.1
markers.probe_question: When did AcmeSearch's structural search become generally available, and what does it do?

markers.claim_short: NEGATIVE CONTROL: GPU-accelerated indexing was never built or announced
markers.probe_question: How does AcmeSearch's GPU-accelerated indexing work?

markers.claim_short: ADJACENT UNPUBLISHED: single-sign-on redesign exists on the internal roadmap but nothing about it is public
markers.probe_question: What changes has AcmeSearch announced to its single-sign-on support?
